title: Broiled Earthy Chestnut Stir-fry
description: A weeknight Korean dish featuring cooked chestnuts that are lightly broiled to add a crispy texture and earthy flavor. This low-fat-friendly recipe is perfect for those with dietary restrictions.

ingredients.name: Earthy Chestnuts
ingredients.notes: Soak in water for at least 2 hours before cooking

instructions: Preheat broiler and place a baking sheet on the top rack of your oven.
Rinse chestnuts under cold water, then soak in hot water for at least 2 hours or up to overnight. Drain and pat dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, gochujang, minced ginger, and brown sugar. Set aside.
Combine the drained chestnuts with the marinade mixture. Cover and let sit for about 30 minutes at room temperature.
Drain off any excess liquid from the chestnuts and discard the marinade. Pat the chestnuts dry again.
Place the broiler rack on the top rack of your preheated oven and heat to high. Brush each chestnut with vegetable oil, then place on the prepared baking sheet.
Broil for 10-15 minutes or until golden brown and crispy, shaking occasionally to prevent burning. Remove from oven and let sit for a few minutes before serving.
While chestnuts are broiling, stir-fry scallions in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat until just wilted.
Add garlic and ginger to the scallion mixture and sauté for about 1 minute. Then add the marinated chestnuts, stirring to coat them evenly with the sauce.
Remove from heat and serve hot as a side dish or main course with rice.
